title: BLEU Score for Translation
concept: BLEU measures n-gram overlap between model translation and human references, checking precision of word sequences.

exercises.prompt: What is a BLEU score used BLEU score primarily is the BLEU score used for?

options.text: To measure how well an AI translates languages.
To check the battery life of translation devices.

exercises.explain: BLEU score is the standard tool used to automatically grade machine translation models by comparing their outputs to professional human translations.

cards.title: How Good is That Translation?
cards.body: When AI translates a sentence from Spanish to English, how do we know if it did a good job? We can't hire human judges to grade millions of translations. That's where the BLEU score steps in as an automated translation judge!

cards.title: What is BLEU?
cards.body: The BLEU score is a metric that evaluates how close an AI-generated translation is to a human-made reference. It does this by checking the overlap of word sequences, called 'n-grams', between the two translations. The score ranges from 0 to 1 (or 0% to 100%).

cards.title: The Recipe Matcher
cards.body: Imagine baking a cake using a recipe. If your cake has the exact same ingredients in the same order as the chef's recipe, it's a perfect match! BLEU score acts like a judge comparing your ingredient list (the AI's translation) to the original chef's list (the human reference).

cards.title: Spotting the Difference
cards.body:
Let's translate a sentence:
• Human reference: "The cat sat on the mat."
• AI Translation A: "The cat sat on the mat." (Perfect match! BLEU = 1.0)
• AI Translation B: "Mat the on sat cat the." (Same words, wrong order! BLEU is very low because the 2-word and 3-word groups don't match).

cards.title: Is 100% Necessary?
cards.body: Many people believe a perfect BLEU score of 1.0 is required for a translation to be useful. In reality, even human translators rarely get a score of 1.0 when compared to each other! A score above 0.4 (40%) is generally considered a very high-quality translation.

cards.title: Comparing Word Orders
cards.body: BLEU checks how well the AI's word groupings match the human's word groupings. The more matches, the higher the score!

left.text: "The small dog barked loudly." (Matches human phrases) -> High BLEU Score

right.text: "Dog barked small the loudly." (Words mixed up) -> Low BLEU Score

exercises.prompt: When measuring BLEU score, what are "n-grams"?

options.text: The speed at which the model translates words.
Sequences of words, like two-word or three-word groups.
The number of punctuation marks in the text.
The font size used in the output.

exercises.explain: N-grams are sequences of 'n' words. For example, a 2-gram (bigram) is a two-word sequence like "the cat". BLEU counts how many of these sequences overlap between the AI and human translations.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The BLEU score is a perfect system that understands the meaning and humor of translations just like a human.
exercises.explain: While BLEU is a great tool, it only checks word overlap. It doesn't understand the deep meaning, context, or artistic nuance of the language like a human does.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe what BLEU score evaluates:
exercises.sentence: BLEU measures the _ of the AI's word sequences against human translations.
exercises.explain: BLEU focuses on precision—making sure the words and phrases generated by the AI actually belong in the correct human reference.

exercises.prompt: Which of these translation outputs would receive a higher BLEU score when compared to the human reference: "Today the sky is blue"?
exercises.explain: The first text is a chaotic jumble of words. The second text has correct, natural English phrasing ("the blue sky") which overlaps better with standard human speech, making it the AI-generated translation that would score higher.

exercises.prompt: Imagine you are building a language translation app. Why would you choose BLEU score to evaluate your model's performance?

options.text: It measures how many of the AI's word sequences match the human references.
It tests if the AI translation uses correct grammatical terminology.
It counts the total number of letters in the AI's sentence.
It rates how emotional or creative the translation sounds.

exercises.explain: BLEU calculates precision by counting how many word groups (n-grams) from the AI's translation actually appear in the human reference translations. It doesn't check grammar rules directly, but matching human word patterns naturally rewards grammatical similarity.
